summary: PR 2592 reorganized and linked several fighter translation units, primarily Kirby copy-special code, by splitting the former ftKb_SpecialNPk.c range into separate Kirby Pikachu, Koopa, Link, and Samus special-N files, updating GALE01 splits/symbol metadata, and marking the new Kirby split objects plus Purin SpecialN as Matching in configure.py. The slice also includes smaller header/include/type/tooling adjustments across fighter, stage, item, and tooling files. Review discussion raised concerns that some split/linkage work relied on externs and possibly premature file splitting, while another commenter argued that linked-code progress outweighed minor regressions.

key_files.role: Adjusted split ranges for Kirby ftKb_Init, ftKb_SpecialN, new Kirby copy-special TUs, and ftkirbyspecialfox.
Changed multiple Kirby and Purin data symbols from global address-style names to local @ labels for strings, floats, and doubles.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: symbols.txt hunks for ftKb_Init_803CB6D8, ftKb_Init_804D93E8, ftPr_Init_804D9C40

key_files.role: Marked new Kirby split TUs and ftPr_SpecialN.c as Matching.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: configure.py MatchingFor Kirby and Purin hunks

key_files.role: Removed the previous large aggregate Kirby copy-special source file.
Added new split source for Kirby Pikachu/Pichu copy-special behavior.
Added new split source for Kirby Koopa copy-special behavior.
Added new split source for Kirby Link copy-special behavior.
Added new split source for Kirby Samus copy-special behavior.
Modified Kirby Fox copy-special source and became a Matching object in configure.py; review objected to string/assert extern usage.
Added includes for ftKb_Init.h and ftkirbyspecialpikachu.h.
Adjusted includes and removed prototypes that moved out with the Pikachu split.
Updated includes, removed two extern float declarations, changed one local type spelling, and removed ftKb_SpecialNPk_800F9FD4 from this file.
Added header for the new Kirby Pikachu special split.
Small source additions and configure.py changed it from NonMatching to Matching.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: configure.py Purin ftPr_SpecialN.c Matching hunk

key_files.role: Fighter forward declaration adjustments.
Fighter type adjustments.
Small source addition.
Small stage header adjustment.
Small stage source adjustment.
Small stage header adjustment.
Small stage/material source adjustment.
Small stage header adjustment.
Small stage header adjustment.
Small item animation-list source addition.
Small tooling shell-script adjustment.

what_changed: Deleted the 2212-line ftKb_SpecialNPk.c aggregate source and introduced separate Kirby copy-special sources for Pikachu/Pichu, Koopa, Link, and Samus.

Updated GALE01 split boundaries so the former ftKb_SpecialNPk range is represented by ftkirbyspecialpikachu.c, ftKb_SpecialNKp.c, ftKb_SpecialNLk.c, and ftKb_SpecialNSs.c, with ftkirbyspecialfox.c gaining .data/.sdata ownership ranges.
Updated configure.py to mark the new Kirby split objects and ftkirbyspecialfox.c as Matching; also changed ftPr_SpecialN.c from NonMatching to Matching.
Changed symbols.txt metadata for several Kirby and Purin string/float/double symbols from global address-style names to local @ labels.
Moved the Pikachu/Pichu entry function out of ftKb_SpecialN.c and removed related prototypes from ftKb_Init.h, with a new ftkirbyspecialpikachu.h added.
Made smaller include/type/header adjustments in fighter, stage, item, and tooling files.

smart_moves.text: The PR split one large Kirby copy-special range into character-specific translation units and wired those splits through both splits.txt and configure.py, which made the linked-code report attribute many matches to the new units.
evidence_refs: text_corpus.jsonl: decomp-dev bot report 106 new matches

smart_moves.text: The PR adjusted symbol scope/labels alongside split ownership changes rather than only changing C files.

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a large fighter TU is split, progress reports may show many 'new matches' in new object names and many 'broken matches' in the old aggregate object; interpret those together as split-attribution churn before treating them as independent regressions.
evidence_refs: text_corpus.jsonl: decomp-dev bot report lists 106 new matches and 98 broken matches, with broken matches under ftKb_SpecialNPk and new matches under split Kirby units

decomp_lessons.lesson: Reviewers may push back on calling a split TU matched when the match depends on externing literals/data out of another file or creating extra data/source files for linkage.
evidence_refs: review_comments.md: 'Why were these changed to externs?'
review_comments.md: 'splitting this file and calling it matched with externs is not the way to go about this'

naming_conventions.convention: New Kirby copy-special files used existing ftKb_SpecialNxx naming for most copied powers, while Pikachu/Pichu used the lower-case existing-style ftkirbyspecialpikachu.c plus ftkirbyspecialpikachu.h.
evidence_refs: changed_files.jsonl: ftKb_SpecialNKp.c, ftKb_SpecialNLk.c, ftKb_SpecialNSs.c, ftkirbyspecialpikachu.c, ftkirbyspecialpikachu.h

naming_conventions.convention: Function names in report excerpts preserve Kirby-copy prefixes such as ftKb_LkSpecialN, ftKb_KpSpecialN, and ftKb_SsSpecialN for Link, Koopa, and Samus copied specials.
evidence_refs: text_corpus.jsonl: bot report new match table

naming_conventions.convention: symbols.txt changes in this PR used local @NNN labels for TU-local literals/floats instead of global ftKb_Init_* or ftPr_Init_* address-style names.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: symbols.txt hunks converting ftKb_Init_804D93E8 and ftPr_Init_804D9C40 series to @ labels

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Split one previously linked Kirby special object range into multiple object files and update split boundaries so each new object can be linked/matched independently.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: splits.txt removes ftKb_SpecialNPk.c text range and adds ftkirbyspecialpikachu.c, ftKb_SpecialNKp.c, ftKb_SpecialNLk.c, ftKb_SpecialNSs.c
raw_diff: configure.py marks those objects Matching

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Adjust data and sdata/sdata2 ownership metadata to follow the new split layout.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: splits.txt ftKb_Init and ftkirbyspecialfox .data/.sdata changes

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Used extern or externalized data/literal references in at least one reviewed hunk, replacing an inline OSReport string and HSD_ASSERT with OSReport(ftKb_Init_803CB6D8); this tactic received reviewer pushback.
Kept PAD_STACK usage in touched code where already present; the slice shows PAD_STACK(0x40) retained in ftKb_SpecialNSpit0_Anim and PAD_STACK(8) in removed ftKb_SpecialNPk_800F9FD4.

review_feedback.author: PsiLupan
review_feedback.comment: Questioned why an inline OSReport string/HSD_ASSERT sequence was changed to an extern-backed OSReport argument.

review_feedback.author: PsiLupan
review_feedback.comment: Said splitting the file and calling it matched with externs was not the right approach.

review_feedback.author: PsiLupan
review_feedback.comment: Said a new data file similarly should not exist.

review_feedback.author: PsiLupan
review_feedback.comment: Stated that Kirby code generally needs more splitting but objected to this linkage approach because it could cause later issues.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.comment: Responded that linking progress outweighed minor regressions and that the regressions were not difficult to fix.

follow_up_queries: Kirby copy special TU split extern literals
ftKb_SpecialNPk split into ftKb_SpecialNKp ftKb_SpecialNLk ftKb_SpecialNSs

evidence_refs: metadata_json: PR metadata, changed file list, counts
raw_diff: truncated but includes primary splits/symbols/configure and representative source hunks
text_corpus.jsonl: bot report, issue comments, review comments
notes: The raw diff is truncated, so detailed contents of the newly added large C files are not fully available in the loaded slice.
The PR body is empty, so author intent beyond the title and diffs is not available.
The bot report provides progress numbers and match attribution, but the intake did not run builds, objdiff, or regression checks.

Review comments mention src/melee/ft/chara/ftKirby/ftKb_SpecialNFx.c and ftKb_SpecialNPk_Data.c, but those paths are not present in the final changed_files list supplied in the slice; treat them as review-context evidence rather than final changed-file facts.
The PR merged despite review concerns; do not infer that the concerns were fully resolved from the slice alone.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Interpret split-TU report churn carefully
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR 2592, splitting ftKb_SpecialNPk into multiple Kirby copy-special TUs produced both many new matches under the new objects and many broken matches under the old aggregate object. Curators may want a reusable lesson that split-attribution churn should be separated from true source regressions when reviewing matching reports.
evidence_refs: text_corpus.jsonl: decomp-dev bot report 106 new matches and 98 broken matches

accepted_candidate_records.title: Extern-backed split matches need scrutiny
accepted_candidate_records.text: Reviewer feedback in PR 2592 objected to calling a split file matched when it relied on externs and questioned replacing inline report/assert text with extern-backed data. This supports a review lesson to scrutinize extern/literal/data-file tactics used to make split TUs link.

source_update_candidates.title: TU split matching should not rely on speculative extern data files
source_update_candidates.text: Consider adding or cross-linking guidance that when splitting a translation unit for matching, reviewers should verify that externed literals/data and added data-only files reflect real split ownership rather than being introduced only to claim a match. PR 2592 review comments specifically objected to extern-backed matching and a data file that 'shouldn't exist.'
source_update_candidates.reason: Existing standards cover data ownership and verification, but this PR supplies historical review evidence for the specific split-TU-with-externs failure mode.

source_update_candidates.title: Kirby copy-special split paths introduced around PR 2592
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2592 evidence shows the former ftKb_SpecialNPk.c range being split into ftkirbyspecialpikachu.c, ftKb_SpecialNKp.c, ftKb_SpecialNLk.c, and ftKb_SpecialNSs.c in GALE01 splits/configure metadata.
source_update_candidates.reason: Useful path-history candidate for future lookup, but should be curated against current repository history before being promoted.

rejection_notes: Do not promote 'linking progress outweighs minor regressions' as a global standard; it is one commenter response and is contradicted by reviewer concern in the same PR.
Do not mark the extern-backed OSReport or added data-file tactic as accepted style; review feedback challenged it and existing standards require data-ownership and verification evidence.
Do not infer that ftKb_SpecialNFx.c or ftKb_SpecialNPk_Data.c were final merged files from the changed_files list, because they appear only in review comments in this slice.
Do not claim all 106 bot-reported matches were independently validated beyond the loaded bot report.
